From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Indian financial newspaper

Not to be confused with [The Economist](/wiki/The_Economist "The Economist") or [Financial Times](/wiki/Financial_Times "Financial Times").

Further details may exist on the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:The_Economic_Times "Talk:The Economic Times"). *(November 2023)* |

The Economic Times

| --- | --- |

| Website | [economictimes.indiatimes.com](http://economictimes.indiatimes.com/) [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q1954757#P856 "Edit this at Wikidata")   ([BFSI vertical](https://bfsi.economictimes.indiatimes.com/)) |
| * [Media of India](/wiki/Media_of_India "Media of India") * [List of newspapers](/wiki/List_of_newspapers_in_India "List of newspapers in India") | |

***The Economic Times*** is an Indian English-language business-focused [daily newspaper](/wiki/Daily_newspaper "Daily newspaper"). [Published](/wiki/Publishing "Publishing") by [The Times Group](/wiki/The_Times_Group "The Times Group"), *The Economic Times* began publication in 1961 and it is sold in all major cities in India. As of 2012, it is the world's second-most widely read English-language [business newspaper](/wiki/Business_newspaper "Business newspaper"), after *[The Wall Street Journal](/wiki/The_Wall_Street_Journal "The Wall Street Journal")*,[[4]](#cite_note-4) with a daily readership of over 800,000.[[5]](#cite_note-5) According to the [Audit Bureau of Circulations](/wiki/Audit_Bureau_of_Circulations_(India) "Audit Bureau of Circulations (India)"), the newspaper's [circulation](/wiki/Print_circulation "Print circulation") averaged 269,882 copies during the latter half of 2022.[[3]](#cite_note-ABCIND-3) It is published simultaneously from 19 cities: [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai"), [Bangalore](/wiki/Bangalore "Bangalore"), [Delhi](/wiki/Delhi "Delhi"), [Chennai](/wiki/Chennai "Chennai"), [Kolkata](/wiki/Kolkata "Kolkata"), [Lucknow](/wiki/Lucknow "Lucknow"), [Hyderabad](/wiki/Hyderabad "Hyderabad"), [Jaipur](/wiki/Jaipur "Jaipur"), [Ahmedabad](/wiki/Ahmedabad "Ahmedabad"), [Nagpur](/wiki/Nagpur "Nagpur"), [Chandigarh](/wiki/Chandigarh "Chandigarh"), [Pune](/wiki/Pune "Pune"), [Patna](/wiki/Patna "Patna"), [Ranchi](/wiki/Ranchi "Ranchi"), [Indore](/wiki/Indore "Indore"), [Bhopal](/wiki/Bhopal "Bhopal"), [Bhubaneshwar](/wiki/Bhubaneshwar "Bhubaneshwar"), [Kochi](/wiki/Kochi "Kochi"), and [Goa](/wiki/Goa "Goa"). Its main content is based on the [Indian economy](/wiki/Economy_of_India "Economy of India"), international finance, share prices, prices of commodities as well as other matters related to finance. The founding editor of the paper when it was launched in 1961 was P. S. Hariharan. The current editor of *The Economic Times* is Bodhisattva Ganguli.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Other ventures

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Economic_Times&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Other ventures")]

In June 2009, *The Economic Times* launched a television channel called [ET Now](/wiki/ET_Now "ET Now").[[7]](#cite_note-7) In 2018, the house of *The Economic Times* launched a member-only platform called ET Prime.[[8]](#cite_note-8) It claims to be a business storytelling platform. ET Prime's current editor (2022) is Shishir Prasad.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

*The Economic Times* has a portfolio management tool called ET Portfolio.[[10]](#cite_note-10) The *Economic Times* also launched ETHRWorld and ETBrandEquity. Author-Publicist Aatish Jaisinghani has also been featured on [Economic Times](/wiki/Economic_Times_Awards "Economic Times Awards") for his take on the shift in audience dynamics and the impact on brand remunerations with National brand campaignsm[[11]](#cite_note-11) as well as thriving in female dominated industries on World PR Day.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

In 2017, *The Economic Times* launched the ET [Hindi](/wiki/Hindi "Hindi") website for the business news in Hindi. In 2022, *The Economic Times* launched its website in the seven other Indian languages, creating: ET [Gujarati](/wiki/Gujarati_language "Gujarati language"); ET [Marathi](/wiki/Marathi_language "Marathi language"); ET [Bengali](/wiki/Bengali_language "Bengali language"); ET [Tamil](/wiki/Tamil_language "Tamil language"); ET [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam"); ET [Telugu](/wiki/Telugu_language "Telugu language"); and ET [Kannada](/wiki/Kannada "Kannada").

## Editors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Economic_Times&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Editors")]

* 1960s and 1970s: P. S. Hariharan (1961–1964),[[13]](#cite_note-13) D. K. Rangnekar (1964–1979) [[14]](#cite_note-14)
* 1980s: Hannan Ezekiel, Manu Shroff (1985–1990) [[15]](#cite_note-15)
* Early to mid 1990s: Jaideep Bose,[[16]](#cite_note-16) T. N. Ninan,[[17]](#cite_note-17) [Swaminathan Anklesaria Aiyar](/wiki/Swaminathan_Anklesaria_Aiyar "Swaminathan Anklesaria Aiyar")
* 2004: Rajrishi Singhal and Rahul Joshi [[18]](#cite_note-18)
* 2010 to 2015: Rahul Joshi[[19]](#cite_note-19)
* 2015 to date: Bodhisatva Ganguli[[20]](#cite_note-20)
